name: chisel
description: Rust-powered precision file operations. Minimal token footprint, strict path confinement.
long_description: Chisel exposes Unix-native file and shell tools to Claude with a fixed path root — no accidental escapes, no full-file reads when a grep will do. Every edit goes through patch_apply (unified diff), every search through shell_exec (grep/rg/sed/find). Typical context cost is 50–160× lower than naive read_file + write_file patterns.

tools.name: patch_apply
tools.description: Apply a unified diff patch to a file atomically. Primary edit tool — sends only changed lines.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write (create or overwrite) a file. Creates parent directories automatically.

tools.name: append
tools.description: Append content to an existing file.

tools.name: move_file
tools.description: Move or rename a file within the root directory.

tools.name: shell_exec
tools.description: Run a whitelisted command: grep sed awk find cat head tail wc sort uniq cut tr diff file stat ls du rg. No shell interpreter — args are passed literally.

root_directory.title: Root Directory
root_directory.description: The folder Chisel is allowed to read and write. All file paths outside this directory are blocked at the kernel level.

read_only.title: Read-Only Mode
read_only.description: When enabled, all write operations are blocked. shell_exec and reads still work.
